title: Reviewer Artifact v1
description: Structured output produced by a single reviewer role. This is the contract between reviewers and the read-only judge stage implemented by aggregate.mjs. The judge consumes ONLY these artifacts; it never reads PR comments or PR reviews. A reviewer that wants a human's inline comment to block must ingest it via `gh api` and fold it into blocking_issues[] with source: "inline_comment". See docs/ci-pipeline.md.

role.description: Adding a role here is a two-step change: reviewer artifacts are validated against main's copy of this schema, so the new value must be merged to main before a PR can emit it.

decision_ref.description: The design-doc decision or finding this blocker cites, e.g. "D6" or "RA-014". The invariant reviewer is expected to populate this: a blocker that cannot name the contract it violates is usually an opinion.

source.description: How this blocker was discovered. `inline_comment` means the reviewer ingested a human's inline PR comment.

id.description: Optional. Present so a fix_suggestions[] entry can reference a note, which fix_suggestions.id already documents as allowed — without this property that reference was unsatisfiable and a reviewer that supplied one failed validation.

decision_ref.description: Same meaning as on a blocking issue. Present here for the same reason `id` is: the invariant reviewer is told to cite the decision a finding relates to, and citing one on an observation it deliberately chose NOT to block on is correct behaviour, not a mistake. Without this property that artifact failed `additionalProperties` and the whole reviewer died — losing every finding it had, including any blockers, over a note it was right to write.

id.description: Should match the id of a blocking_issues[] or non_blocking_notes[] entry when this suggestion addresses one.

followup_issues.description: Tightly-scoped follow-up issues a human may choose to open. Loose scoping risks an issue/PR loop.
